Name: Claude Code
OneLiner: Terminal/IDE agentic coder that plans, edits files, runs commands and iterates in a loop.
BestFor: Multi-file changes and refactors across a real repo
Tasks where the agent must run tests and fix until green
Using MCP servers and subagents for larger jobs
NotIdealFor: Working with zero oversight on critical code
TokenTips: Use /clear or compaction between unrelated tasks to drop stale context
Add a CLAUDE.md so it stops re-discovering project facts each run
Point it at specific files instead of letting it scan the whole tree

Name: GitHub Copilot
OneLiner: Inline autocomplete and chat inside your editor, strongest at in-the-moment suggestions.
BestFor: Fast inline completions as you type
Quick chat about the file you're in
NotIdealFor: Autonomous multi-step tasks across many files
Long-running agent loops with tool use
TokenTips: Keep only relevant files open - Copilot uses open tabs as context
Accept partial suggestions and guide rather than regenerating repeatedly

Name: OpenAI Codex / CLI
OneLiner: OpenAI's agentic coding tool/CLI for delegating coding tasks to a model.
BestFor: Delegating a scoped task and reviewing the diff
NotIdealFor: Inline as-you-type completion (use an IDE assistant)
TokenTips: Give a tight task scope and target files to avoid broad exploration
Reuse a stable system/setup prompt to benefit from caching

Name: Cline
OneLiner: Open-source VS Code agent extension; bring your own API key and model.
BestFor: Open, model-agnostic agentic coding inside VS Code
Using your own keys (Claude, GPT, local models)
TokenTips: Pick a cheaper model for planning, a stronger one for hard edits
Prune the conversation when switching tasks

Name: Aider
OneLiner: Terminal pair-programmer that edits your git repo and commits changes.
BestFor: Targeted edits from the command line
NotIdealFor: GUI-first users who don't live in the terminal
TokenTips: Use /add to include only the files you need in the chat context
Use the repo map instead of dumping whole files

Name: Ollama
OneLiner: Run open-weight models locally with one command; private and offline.
BestFor: Private/offline inference on your own machine
Serving local models to other tools (Cline, scripts)
NotIdealFor: Machines with little RAM/VRAM (use small quantized models)
TokenTips: Choose a quantized (e.g. 4-bit GGUF) model to fit your hardware
Smaller models = larger effective throughput for simple tasks

Name: LangChain / LlamaIndex
OneLiner: Libraries for wiring up RAG pipelines, agents and tool use in your own app.
BestFor: Building custom RAG or agent apps in code
Swapping models/vector stores behind one interface
NotIdealFor: Simple one-off scripts (often raw SDK is simpler)
TokenTips: Retrieve top-k tightly and rerank - don't stuff the whole corpus
Cache embeddings; only re-embed changed documents

Name: LiteLLM
OneLiner: One API gateway in front of 100+ model providers, with cost/usage tracking.
BestFor: Central budgeting, rate-limiting and logging of token spend
NotIdealFor: Single-provider apps that don't need a proxy
TokenTips: Use its spend tracking to find your most expensive prompts
Route cheap tasks to cheap models automatically
